creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
OpenSearch
==========


Bugzilla
--------

The page does not yet mention the extension for FreeBSD Bugzilla. 


MarkMail
--------

<https://www.freebsd.org/search/opensearch/markmail.xml> 

* not found. 

For this type of engine to appear in the address bar of Firefox, it's necessary to visit the site to be searched. Please see: 

Add or remove a search engine in Firefox | Firefox Help
<https://support.mozilla.org/kb/add-or-remove-search-engine-firefox>

Deprecation of OpenSearch XML add-ons & current users
<https://discourse.mozilla.org/t/-/46868?u=grahamperrin>


Non-OpenSearch
==============

<https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/search/?category=search-tools&sort=recommended%2Cusers&type=extension> redirects to 
<https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/extensions/category/search-tools/>. 

Better to refer to the more modern (and de-localised) form of URL: 
<https://addons.mozilla.org/extensions/category/search-tools/>

In any case: a link to add-ons of this type (type: extension) no longer fits in a page that is OpenSearch-specific. 


Generally
=========

Given the deprecation, it may be appropriate to change the URL; have a redirect; and change the title the page, for non-OpenSearch -related content to fit. Think of this as a minor overhaul :-)
